content.problemStatement:
Given NNN pairs of parentheses. Find all **valid parentheses** string of length 2⋅N2 \cdot N2⋅N.

Note: A **valid parentheses** string is defined as:


Empty string is valid.
If PPP is valid, (P)(P)(P) is also valid.
If PPP and QQQ are valid, PQPQPQ is also valid.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output M+1M+1M+1 lines, where MMM is the number of valid parentheses:



The first line contains a single integer MMM.
The next MMM lines contain string of length 2⋅N2 \cdot N2⋅N.

Note: The valid parentheses strings must be printed in **lexicographically increasing** order.
String SSS is said to be lexicographically smaller than string TTT if there exists a position iii where Si<TiS_i \lt T_iSi​<Ti​ and Sj=TjS_j = T_jSj​=Tj​ for all j<ij \lt ij<i.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the number of valid parentheses strings of length 2*N on the first line, followed by each string on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: There will be 2 valid parenthesis strings (()) and ()().
Test case 2: There will be 5 valid parenthesis strings ((())), (()()), (())(), ()(()), and ()()().

Explanation:
Test case 1: There will be 1 valid parenthesis string ()

Explanation:
Test case 1: There will be 14 valid parenthesis strings

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using a backtracking approach. We start with an empty string and add either an open or close parenthesis at each step. We ensure that the number of close parentheses never exceeds the number of open parentheses. If we reach a point where we have added 2*N parentheses, we add the current string to our result list.
solution.timeComplexity: O(4^N / N^(3/2)) per test case, O(T * 4^N / N^(3/2)) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(4^N / N^(3/2)) — the dominant structure is the list of valid parentheses strings.
